title: Inheritance Windfall: Integrating a Sudden Wealth Event
goals: Deploy $395,000 inheritance wisely without making costly mistakes
Manage inherited IRA 10-year distribution rule efficiently
Catch up on 529 college funding for two children
Avoid windfall paralysis and create actionable deployment plan
constraints: Inherited IRA 10-year distribution rule — mandatory complete distribution by year 10
Highly appreciated inherited brokerage positions — step-up basis window closing
3.25% mortgage creates prepayment vs. investment dilemma
Windfall paralysis — 8 months elapsed without any action
key_risks: Inherited IRA 25% excise tax penalty if 10-year rule violated
Tax spike risk if all inherited IRA distributed in final years
Step-up in basis window closing on inherited brokerage positions
No umbrella liability insurance at $706K net worth

planning_issues.description: Inherited IRA 10-year rule: no distributions taken in year 1 (8 months elapsed); bunching distributions creates tax spike risk
Inherited taxable brokerage received step-up in basis at death; near-zero capital gains if sold now vs. growing gains if held
$12,000 in 529 plans vs $160-300K needed for two children; 4-year runway for oldest
$168,000 inheritance sitting in money market — opportunity cost; deployment framework needed

key_recommendations: Begin inherited IRA distributions immediately: $13,500-17,500/year over 10 years
Sell inherited brokerage within 30 days — realize step-up in basis; reinvest in diversified index funds
Cash deployment priority: emergency fund top-up → car loan payoff → 529 superfunding → retirement maximization → taxable brokerage
Do NOT prepay 3.25% mortgage — invest surplus at expected 7% long-term return instead
Purchase $2M umbrella liability policy at $706K+ net worth
follow_up_questions: Exact date of aunt's death? (Starts 10-year inherited IRA clock)
Has a CPA modeled the inherited IRA annual distribution schedule and tax impact?
Specific holdings in inherited brokerage? (Affects step-up calculation and rebalancing approach)
Does William's government employer offer a 457b plan? (No early withdrawal penalty — powerful supplement)
outcome_summary: Inherited brokerage sold within 45 days (minimal capital gains). 10-year inherited IRA schedule established at $14,000/year. Both 529 plans superfunded to $25,000 each. Car loan paid off.
key_lesson: Sudden wealth recipients need a structured decision framework — prioritizing time-sensitive decisions first (inherited IRA schedule, step-up basis window) transforms a windfall from anxiety into financial acceleration.
